reasoning: Query asks for Buddhist temple lodging (shukubo). 87 results returned but top items are unrelated: a ryokan in Iwate, a Saga tourism portal, and a Nagano article about Zenkoji area lodging. One relevant spot (Tamashoin, Nagano) appears in result 3 but is buried. Core intent partially addressed but useful signal is submerged under noise.
missing_data: Specific named shukubo with booking info, rules, types of activities (zazen, morning service, sutra copying), location, and price range. Koyasan (Mount Koya) is the archetypal shukubo destination and does not appear.
wrong_data: Top result '中斋旅馆' is a Chinese-language ryokan result, completely off-topic. Saga tourism portal also unrelated.
improvement_hint: Add category tag 'shukubo' or 'temple_lodging' to relevant spots. Boost ranking for spots with 宿坊 keyword in name/description. Index Koyasan shukubo cluster.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for onsen towns with yukata-strolling culture. Results return 79 matches but none clearly describe the specific yukata street-walking experience. Nozawa Onsen and Shinonoi appear but without yukata/geta street context. No results mention the specific aesthetic (toroji/rotemburo hopping in yukata) the traveler wants.
missing_data: Named onsen machi with yukata-walk culture: Kinosaki Onsen, Nozawa Onsen, Yufuin, Zao. Details on public foot-baths (sotenburo), yukata rental, inter-bath walkability.
improvement_hint: Add 'yukata_walk_friendly' or 'onsen_machi' attribute to qualifying spots. Keyword-boost queries combining 浴衣+温泉街 or 外湯巡り to surface Kinosaki and similar destinations first.

reasoning: French query for whale watching (baleines/クジラ) returns 87 results with zero relevant hits visible. Top results include Kyoto riverboat info, Monet's garden in Kochi, Miyazaki site policy page, an Ishikawa park, and Okayama fruit agriculture. No whale watching spots appear. Complete retrieval failure.
missing_data: Ogasawara whale watching, Kochi (Tosa-wan) whale watching, Zamami Island (Okinawa), Shiretoko (Hokkaido). Season info, tour operator details, booking windows.
wrong_data: All top results are entirely unrelated to whale watching or cetaceans.
improvement_hint: Index whale watching (ホエールウォッチング) explicitly as a category. Map クジラ keyword to known whale-watching destinations: Ogasawara, Kochi, Okinawa, Hokkaido. Add lang=fr fallback to Japanese-keyword search.

reasoning: Tool fetches Kyoto Japan Heritage items. The first result—800-year tea history walk in Yamashiro/Uji—is highly relevant and rich: mentions Uji tea gardens, chakin (tea ceremony implements), specific sites like Tsuen tea house (24th generation) and Okuno-yama Chagarden. The second result (Chinjufu naval heritage) is off-topic. Data is official Cultural Affairs agency material with good specificity.
missing_data: Specific tea ceremony experience venues (not just history sites). Distinction between tourist performance vs. serious practitioner experience. Urasenke or Omotesenke school context.
improvement_hint: Add a theme filter for 'tea_ceremony_experience' to get_japan_heritage or link to get_spots results for Uji/Kyoto matcha experience venues. Second result (naval heritage) is irrelevant noise for this prefecture query—improve relevance ranking.

reasoning: Query about mountain villages with infrequent train service (local rail atmosphere). Tool returns 89 results for ローカル but top items are: Hiroshima city admin page, Iwate tourism link policy, Kanagawa cookie notice page, and an Aomori DMO plan mentioning local railroads. No actual named villages with scenic local train service surfaced.
missing_data: Specific named local railways and villages: Iida Line (Nagano), Tadami Line (Fukushima), Yoichino-Mori (Iwate), Nishiki river (Shimane). Train frequency, scenic attributes, accommodation availability.
wrong_data: Top results are admin/legal pages with no travel content.
improvement_hint: Create a 'scenic_railway' or 'local_train_village' category. Scrape DMO pages specifically about ローカル鉄道観光. The Aomori DMO chunk about local railroads in chunk 17 should be promoted to top results for this query type.

reasoning: Query asks for non-mainstream traditional food culture. Tool returns 507 GI-designated foods starting with Aomori Cassis, Tajima Beef, Kobe Beef, Yubari Melon—all famous products. The data is accurate and well-structured, but the ranking fails the constraint: user wants obscure/unknown foods, yet the list starts with the most famous items. Kobe Beef (#3) is the opposite of 'not mainstream'.
missing_data: Fermented foods (narezushi, funazushi, shottsuru), regional miso variants, unusual preserved foods. No filtering mechanism for 'obscure' vs. 'famous' items within the GI list.
improvement_hint: Add a 'mainstream_score' or 'recognition_level' field to GI items. Allow filtering by designation recency or regional obscurity. Sort by least-known items first when user signals interest in undiscovered foods.

reasoning: Identical data as L4-01 but in Japanese. Same constraint failure: query asks for not-mainstream food culture but first items are Aomori Cassis, Tajima Beef, Kobe Beef, Yubari Melon—all extremely well-known. Japanese language rendering is accurate. Same structural ranking problem.
missing_data: Obscure fermented products, regional preserved foods, niche GI items from remote prefectures that most Japanese people haven't heard of.
improvement_hint: Same as L4-01: implement relevance sorting that deprioritizes globally famous brand foods when query signals 'not mainstream'. Consider curating a 'hidden gem foods' subcategory.

reasoning: Query asks for forgotten craftsman skills. Tool returns traditional arts list starting with Nogaku (Noh theater), then festivals (Kifune, Okumakabuto, Yobuko tug-of-war, Kawagoe Hikawa Festival)—not craftsman skills at all. The intent needs craft/techniques (weaving, lacquer, pottery) but gets performing arts and festivals. Category mismatch.
missing_data: Craft techniques: Edo kiriko glass cutting, Nanbu ironwork, Bizen pottery, traditional paper-making (washi), Maki-e lacquerwork. Venues where dying crafts can be observed or learned.
improvement_hint: Add category=craft filter to get_traditional_arts. Current results mix performing arts, festivals, and crafts without distinction. Query for 'craftsman skills' should map to 工芸 subcategory, not 民俗文化財 festivals.

reasoning: Query asks for Hokusai-like landscapes (Mount Fuji views, dramatic seacoasts, etc.). Tool searches 富士 and returns 103 results including Yamanashi nature pages (Fuji-specific), Fujikawaguchiko, Fujiyoshida, Fujinomiya—genuinely relevant. However many results are administrative municipalities or generic tourism portals without specific viewpoints. The Yamanashi nature page does describe Fuji viewspots. Fukui brochure page is off-topic noise.
missing_data: Specific named Fuji viewpoints (Chureito Pagoda, Kawaguchiko, Oshino Hakkai), coastal ukiyo-e landscapes (Tago no Ura, Enoshima), non-Fuji Hokusai locations (Kanagawa wave viewpoint, Kajikazawa in Kofu).
improvement_hint: Add 'viewpoint' attribute to spots with famous landscape photography. Cross-reference Japan Heritage 富士山 story for named scenic vantage points. Filter out administrative municipality entries when user intent is scenic spots.

reasoning: Query about lesser-known UNESCO sites uses get_japan_heritage across all prefectures. First result is Japan Heritage story 001 (Early Modern Education Heritage: Ashikaga Gakko, Shizutani, Kangien, Kodokan)—legitimately lesser-known with rich detail. Data comes from Agency for Cultural Affairs, well-structured with prefecture codes, periods, themes. 104 total items provide good coverage of the 104 Japan Heritage stories.
missing_data: UNESCO World Heritage distinctions vs. Japan Heritage (Bunka-cho) distinctions—tool conflates them. Actual UNESCO WH sites like Iwami Ginzan or Hiraizumi might be what user wants, but are not explicitly surfaced.
wrong_data: Japan Heritage stories are NOT UNESCO sites—they are a separate Japanese national designation. Tool response conflates the two, which could mislead an agent.
improvement_hint: Add a data_type field clearly distinguishing Japan Heritage (文化庁) from UNESCO World Heritage. Add a 'lesser_known_score' to help agents pick genuinely obscure items vs. Nikko or Kyoto temples.

reasoning: Query asks where Jomon-era culture still influences daily life. Results include good spots: Nagano Kuroyoseishi Museum (hands-on Jomon experience), Yamanashi Jomon pottery sites, Kuroyu Obsidian Museum. However the first result is a Nara school trip page (off-topic). Useful spots appear but coverage of actual Jomon cultural continuity (not just museums) is sparse.
missing_data: Sanai Maruyama (Aomori) Jomon site, Kazuno stone circles, examples of Jomon design influence on contemporary crafts, Hokkaido Jomon living culture. 'Daily life influence' aspect mostly missing.
improvement_hint: Add '縄文' as a heritage tag with geographic clustering around known Jomon archaeological zones: Aomori, Nagano, Yamanashi, Hokkaido. Surface experiential content over administrative pages.

reasoning: Chinese query about regional fermented food culture. Tool returns 507 GI foods in Chinese translation—translations appear accurate (Aomori Cassis→青森黑加仑, Tajima Beef→但马牛). However the same ranking problem as L4-01/02: fermented food specialist request returns Kobe Beef and Yubari Melon at top. 507 items contain fermented products but they're buried. Constraint on fermentation theme completely ignored.
missing_data: Specific fermented foods: narezushi (funazushi of Shiga), shottsuru (Akita), Kyoto tsukemono, nukazuke, amazake, natto varieties by region, regional miso types.
improvement_hint: Add fermentation/preservation category filtering. Tag GI items with 発酵/fermented attribute so Chinese-language users can discover funazushi, shottsuru, tsukemono GI items specifically.

reasoning: Query about mountain villages with shamanic traditions (itako, yamabushi). Tool returns general traditional arts list starting with Nogaku, then folk festival list: Kifune, Okumakabuto, Yobuko tug-of-war. None relate to shamanism (itako, yugyo, yamabushi, mountain asceticism). Complete mismatch between query intent and results.
missing_data: Osorezan (Aomori) itako medium culture, Dewa Sanzan (Yamagata) yamabushi asceticism, Kunisaki Peninsula (Oita) mountain Buddhist traditions, Haguro-san pilgrimage details.
improvement_hint: Add 山岳信仰/shamanism/修験道 as tags for relevant heritage entries. Osorezan and Dewa Sanzan should be discoverable via get_traditional_arts with category=folk or via search_area. Currently these critical spots are not surfaced.

reasoning: Query about atomic bomb history in daily landscape. Results include: one genuine hit (Hiroshima city atomic bombing page) and one Kamaishi (tsunami/disaster resilience) spot. However the first two results are Yamagata tourism pages in Chinese/traditional Chinese (irrelevant), and Kumamoto about page. Only the Hiroshima hit is squarely on topic. Nagasaki missing entirely from visible results.
missing_data: Nagasaki atomic bomb museum, Nagasaki Peace Park, Urakami Cathedral. Hiroshima A-bomb dome, living survivor testimonies program. Peace tourism context for both cities.
improvement_hint: Tag Hiroshima and Nagasaki peace/atomic heritage spots with 原爆/平和 category for direct retrieval. Current results surface irrelevant Chinese-language tourism pages first due to keyword coincidence.

reasoning: Query about towns built around single craft industry matches well. get_local_specialty with category=craft returns 231 METI-designated traditional crafts. Results show Nibutani Attus (Hokkaido Ainu weaving, Biratori Town), Oitama Tsumugi (Yamagata, textile town), Ugo Shina Cloth (Tsuruoka area). These are specific named crafts with production areas, authority designation, translated descriptions. Agent can build a useful answer.
missing_data: Missing the 'town identity' layer—which towns are *dominated* by a single craft (e.g., Arita for porcelain, Nishijin for weaving, Echizen for paper). Craft descriptions present but town cultural identity context is thin.
improvement_hint: Add a 'craft_town' flag for municipalities where a single craft defines local identity (e.g., Arita=porcelain, Tsubame-Sanjo=metalwork). This would enable better narrative answers about single-craft towns.

reasoning: Query about industrial decline preservation. Results include: Kagoshima industrial heritage page (Shuseikan/Meiji industrial revolution heritage), Nagoya industrial tourism DMO page, Gunma DMO plan mentioning Tomioka Silk Mill. Three reasonably relevant results among noise. However 'industrial decline' aspect (abandoned mines, rusted factories repurposed) is not well-addressed—results focus on Meiji-era industrial *success* not decline.
missing_data: Abandoned coal mines (Hashima Island/Gunkanjima, Yubari), Kamaishi steel ruins, post-industrial art spaces, North Kyushu industrial heritage tour. Distinction between preserved success vs. preserved decline.
improvement_hint: Add 'industrial_heritage' category with subcategories: meiji_revolution, post_industrial_decline, mining, textile. Hashima Island (Gunkanjima) is a canonical answer to this query and should appear prominently.

reasoning: Korean query about Japan's religious diversity. Tool returns Japan Heritage in Korean—translated well. But first item is Early Modern Education Heritage (not about religious diversity). The 104-item dataset would contain relevant items about Shinto-Buddhist syncretism, but they're buried. Korean translation quality appears decent. Constraint (religious diversity) is not served by the default ordering.
missing_data: Miyajima (Shinto-Buddhist coexistence), Nikko (syncretism), Nagasaki hidden Christian sites, Ise Jingu, Koyasan (Shingon Buddhism), Ainu spiritual sites. Explicit religious diversity framing missing.
improvement_hint: Add theme=religion filter to get_japan_heritage. Items covering 神仏習合, 隠れキリシタン, Ainu spirituality should be front-loaded for religion-diversity queries. Korean language support confirmed working.

reasoning: Query asks specifically for dying/remote festivals. get_festivals returns 81 items: Kifune (Kanagawa), Okumakabuto (Noto/Ishikawa), Kawagoe Hikawa (Saitama), Tatemon (Toyama), Yasurai (null description). These are all Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties—officially designated—which suggests they're recognized as at-risk. However 'remote Japan' and 'dying' aspects aren't distinguished. Kawagoe is suburban Tokyo, not remote.
missing_data: Information on actual attendance decline, depopulation threat, or 'last few practitioners' status. Truly remote micro-festivals with dwindling participants. Date fields mostly null—critical for planning.
improvement_hint: Add 'remoteness_score' and 'endangerment_status' to festival items. Municipalities with population under 5,000 hosting festivals should be flagged. Dates are critical and mostly null—this is a significant data gap.

reasoning: Query about 2011 tsunami-shaped coastal areas. Results include: Sendai symposium page (震災15年), Noto Peninsula 2024 earthquake recovery minshuku story (adjacent context), Kamaishi DMC disaster prevention programs, Rikuzentakata tsunami memorial, Kesennuma DMO plan with 2011 recovery narrative. Strong relevant hits. Kamaishi and Kesennuma are exactly the answer. Noto content is about 2024 earthquake, slightly off-topic but close.
missing_data: Specific itinerary for Sanriku coast disaster-learning route. Opening hours of tsunami memorials. Comparison of 2011 recovery stages across Miyako, Ofunato, Minamisanriku.
wrong_data: Noto Peninsula content refers to 2024 Noto earthquake, not 2011 Tohoku—agents must be careful to distinguish.
improvement_hint: Tag Sanriku coast spots with '2011_tsunami' event tag vs. '2024_noto_earthquake' to avoid confusion. Surfaced results are genuinely strong for this query; main improvement is temporal tagging.

reasoning: Query about Meiji-era giyofu (pseudo-Western) architecture. Search returns 84 results for 擬洋風 but top results are: Fukui Chinese/Taiwanese tourism portal, Miyazaki Chinese tourism portal, Yamagata traditional Chinese, and Saga introductions. Zero architectural content visible in truncated results. Retrieval completely fails for niche architectural term.
missing_data: Kaichi School (Matsumoto, Nagano)—the canonical giyofu building. Tomioka Silk Mill, Hokkaido Old Government Building (Sapporo), Iwasaki Villa (Tokyo). Meiji architecture museum clusters.
wrong_data: All visible results are multilingual tourism portal home pages with no relation to Meiji architecture.
improvement_hint: Add architectural heritage tags including '擬洋風建築', '明治建築', 'Meiji_western_architecture'. Kaichi School, Hokkaido Government Building, and similar should be tagged. Currently no architectural period metadata exists in scrape results.

reasoning: Query about hidden Christian heritage in Japan. Tool correctly targets Nagasaki prefecture and returns 8 relevant Japan Heritage items. First result covers Iki, Tsushima, and Goto Islands with rich body text. The Goto Islands and Nagasaki are the canonical hidden Christian (kakure kirishitan) heritage areas. Data is from Agency for Cultural Affairs, well-translated, with detailed narrative. Excellent tool choice and response quality.
missing_data: The Nagasaki Sotome coast, Ikitsuki Island kakure kirishitan villages, and specific churches (Ooura Cathedral, Shitsu Church) would enrich the response. The 2018 UNESCO 'Hidden Christian Sites in the Nagasaki Region' is a better match but may not be in corpus.
improvement_hint: Cross-reference Japan Heritage stories with UNESCO World Heritage 'Hidden Christian Sites' designation (2018) for Nagasaki. Second result (Kitaso Four Cities/Edo journey) is irrelevant to hidden Christianity—ranking for prefecture-specific queries needs refinement.

reasoning: Query asks for gardens by lesser-known garden masters. Results include: Kokemushiro moss garden (Ehime, unique), Hokkaido DMO event page (irrelevant), Hokuho Cultural Museum (Niigata—mentions garden by Tanaka Taiami, a real lesser-known designer—this is a direct hit!), Nagano-Niigata Garden Road article. One genuine hit for lesser-known designer (Tanaka Taiami at Hokuho Museum) but constraint 'lesser-known masters' is only accidentally served.
missing_data: More named garden designers beyond Ogawa Jihei and famous names. Tanaka Taiami garden (Niigata) is the only named match. Gardens in Shimane, Yamaguchi, Tokushima prefectures by regional masters.
improvement_hint: Add 'garden_designer' attribute to garden spots. Tag designers with their fame level. Currently no structured garden-designer metadata exists making this constraint nearly unservable without curated tags.

reasoning: Query about textile dyeing experience. Returns 231 craft items starting with weaving (Nibutani Attus, Oitama Tsumugi with natural dye variants, Ugo Shina Cloth). Oitama Tsumugi's 草木染紬 (plant-dyed silk) is directly relevant. Data is accurate and specific with production areas. However 'experience' aspect (workshops, studios you can visit) is absent—only product designations, not experience venues.
missing_data: Kyoto Yuzen dyeing workshops, indigo dyeing (awa-ai, Tokushima), Kyoto Nishijin weaving experience studios, Kyushu Bingata (Okinawa). Actual workshop locations with booking information.
improvement_hint: Add 'experience_available' boolean to craft items where visitor dyeing workshops exist. Link METI craft designations to experiential venues in the same production area. Current data is strong on products but silent on visitor experiences.

reasoning: Query about Edo highway culture remnants. Results include: Nagano Yanagi-machi post town (excellent detail—Hokkoku Kaido, 200-year-old machiya, specific cafes/restaurants), Kiso Nakasen-do Suhara-juku and Nojiri-juku article (strong), then noise (Hiroshima admin page, Yamagata tourism portal). Two strong hits with specific named post towns and living culture details. However only 2 out of 5 visible results are relevant.
missing_data: Magome-juku and Tsumago-juku (famous Kiso Valley), Hakone-juku, Kusatsu-juku. The Tokaido and Nikko Kaido post town networks. More coverage of the Five Routes of Edo.
improvement_hint: Tag post-town (宿場町) spots explicitly. Kiso Valley Nakasen-do cluster should appear prominently for this query. Admin and multilingual homepage results should be deprioritized in favor of content-rich post-town articles.

reasoning: Query asks for spiritual pilgrimages beyond Shikoku 88. Tool returns all 104 Japan Heritage stories starting with the Early Modern Education Heritage—completely unrelated to pilgrimage. The 104 items likely contain Kumano Kodo and Dewa Sanzan pilgrimage stories but they're not surfaced. Constraint (pilgrimage routes that are NOT Shikoku 88) is completely ignored.
missing_data: Kumano Kodo (World Heritage pilgrimage), Dewa Sanzan (Yamagata), Saigoku 33 Kannon pilgrimage, Shikoku 88 adjacent lesser-known routes, Bandai-san pilgrimage, Nishi-Nihon pilgrimage routes.
improvement_hint: Add theme=pilgrimage filter to get_japan_heritage. The Kumano Kodo Japan Heritage story should be retrievable directly. For pilgrimage queries, filter by 巡礼/pilgrimage/信仰 themes rather than returning all 104 stories in default order.
